entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content:
I think Dev would have to be certain there was a bug, and they would have to know the difference between what I created and what you created so they can find out what use case does the flow function fail at, if at all. 

Also, I'm not sure you said what landscape you are using.

The only interesting this is that apparently this flow function does not modify the data in the variable, but only in the UI.

entry.title: Re: validate an input field in SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
Use formula like this:

 

IF(MATCHES_REGEX("1234567890123a", "^[0-9]{12}$"),"MATCH","BOOOOOOOOOOOO!")

 

 Obviously, replaces the "123..." string with your variable.

P.S.: Thanks, ChatGPT 😺

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA Error while passing payload using postman - p...
entry.content: @arroyoweisson Did you need something?

entry.title: Santa builds process to distribute Christmas presents
entry.content:
No one would deny that Santa has a complex process to give out presents for Christmas. 

But several key new SAP Build Process Automation features has enabled Santa to automate the process using a true Fusion Team of reindeer, elves, and his wife. Among the features:

  • Scheduled triggers
  • Script task
  • Custom variables
  • Subprocesses
  • Go to task

And the process can actually teach us some of the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation.

Scheduled Triggers

Probably the most obvious feature to help Santa is the scheduled trigger for regular processes. We could rely on one of the elves to send an API call, but the scheduled trigger is much more reliable.

Action and Data Type to Get Children 

The process uses an action to retrieves a list of children.

We first created a data type to represent a child (name string and a Boolean on whether they were nice or naughty).

Then we created custom variables, one to hold the full list of children, and another to hold the current child in our iteration.

Then we run an action to get the children.

Script Task and GoTo for Iterating Children

Now we create a script task to move the last child into the current child variable, and to remove that child from the full list.

And finally we create an iteration sequence by a Go To back to the start, where we have a condition to see if the list has more children, and 

Subprocesses

We create subprocesses like "Prepare Reindeer and Sled" since we are likely to need to do that during the year, and we create subprocesses like "Deliver via Chimney" in order to simplify and isolate our logic.

Obvious, in a more realistic case we would have created additional logic to review all the actions of a child to determine if they were nice or naughty, as Santa "sees you when you're sleepin'" and "knows when you're awake", and likely is aware of all actions of a children, so we might use a Decision to make that determination.

And we might create an automation to deal with the complexity of building the toys, handling emails in Outlook from kids wanting presents, and looking for sales on toys on the web. 

🎅🎄❄🤶⛪🕎🕌🤗🦌

Hoping the holiday season and the new year brings you peace and love and happiness, and everything you dream about.

entry.title: Re: validate an input field in SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
The error message explains the issue.

i assume you have an IF flow function, so you just need to return Boolean. You just need the MATCH_REGEX without the IF function

entry.title: Re: How to use the multiselect combobox feature
entry.content:
There seems to be a bug, or at least some design I do not understand.

If you open the multi combo box up -- at least on my system, there is an event triggered every time the selection changes, the result is the selected property is reset to false for all of them.

I disconnected this logic and it worked as expected for me.

It's possible I did something to my version of the component, but it's my trial account so it seems not so likely.

entry.title: Re: Change in "Universal REST API integration" in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
If you display the data in your data variable, you will find it empty, which explains why you don't see anything further along. There is a fundamental problem, which is you are executing a list, but you are returning an object (see your first screenshot).

There are 2 ways to do this:

  1. Fix the current way.
  2. Probably use the right data source.

To fix the current project using a REST data resource, you need to return a list in your list action. SO you have o get rid of everything else besides your list in the response. To do this, use the response mapper. It already has the basic coding, so you just add "value" to be the top level to be returned in the response.

{records: response.data.value, driverCode: response.status}

 

Now you use he data variable for the repeat, and the bind the fields to "data items in repeat". Not sure what you are doing with the nesting repeats, since there are no nested objects in the response.

But all his begs the question: Why not use the OData data resource for this OData API?

In the past people wanted to send complex OData filters, and this was not possible with the OData data resource, but now you can define actions, which will let you do all that, so I would think that is the way o go.

entry.title: Re: Include Logo on Request Form in SBPA
entry.content:
Do you mean forms that appear in the inbox (notifications and approvals)?

You can now create UI5 forms instead of using the built in editor, in which case you make anything you want. There is no feature like in Work Zone where you can sub in your own logo to appear in the corner.

I haven't seen any other backlog item about this.

entry.title: Re: Get Requests data in F4 help
entry.content: Are you asking you want people to press F4, or are you saying you want the fields populated from an action, or are you saying you want it populated from an action that runs dynamically during the run of the instance?

entry.title: Re: Filter Get Request Data
entry.content: Why can't you use the output of one action as an input of another action?

entry.title: Re: How to use the multiselect combobox feature
entry.content: Are you able to accept the solution?

entry.title: Re: Change in "Universal REST API integration" in SAP Bui...
entry.content: No worries, and no reason to explain. I'm glad I can help on your journey. Please continue to ask questions -- that is what the community is for. Your post shows SAP has to do a better job of explaining how to use the product.

entry.title: Re: Unable to activate Account for Identity Authentication Service via email link
entry.content: Are you saying you took a new trial account, ran the booster for Build Apps, which then created Cloud Identity Service and sent you an email, but the link in the email does not work?

entry.title: Re: Unable to activate Account for Identity Authenticatio...
entry.content: If you open the Cloud Identity Service instance, you should be taken to the same place. Does this work for you?

entry.title: Re: How to Connect SAP Build Apps to Demo S/4HANA Data
entry.content:
You can connect any REST/OData API, and through a destination you handle any type of authentication to those APIs that destinations support. For more complex scenarios Integration Suite (API Management) can be used.

I'm not familiar with how you created the API and what authentication it requires so I couldn't say specifically. I've created CDS views and connected to them with basic authentication, but you likely why to create trust between your BTP and your S/4 system, and that's beyond my expertise.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App deployment
entry.content:
Is this what you are looking for? After selecting space, I selected instances.

Or are you not seeing them under HTML5 Applications because it says something like this?

entry.title: Re: Unable to consume data from a odata service
entry.content: What error code and message are returned when retrieving data in the UI?

entry.title: Re: Unable to consume data from a odata service
entry.content:
Do you have a real S/4HANA that you are connecting with the plain OData data resource, putting in your credentials in the flow function? Generally, people would create a destination and then use the OData destination data source.

I imagine you are getting a CORS error and no data is returned. But whenever you don't get data, you should retrace your steps and check at each step if data appears. For example, is data returned to the data variable before getting to the UI.

entry.title: Re: Unable to consume data from a odata service
entry.content:
I just add an alert after the get record collection in the data variable (or wherever you are doing the fetching) to display the output and make sure I am getting data. I imagine now for you you are not getting since it is a CORS issue.

Can you show the details of your destination, including the full URL (except for the credentials 😉)?

entry.title: Understand DMS API – Free Postman Collection!!
entry.content:
In my videos introducing the Document Management Service, I received some comments asking for the Postman Collection I used to show how the API works.

So I decided to release Postman collection, and write a short blog post to explain how the APIs work.

You can see all my DMS videos in the DMS playlist - more to come soon: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hLrCoHPwqT4&list=PL6RpkC85SLQCsKSvIOqMzFKW30B4TKXh-

 

How the APIs Work

There's 2 API Sets

The APIs are described on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, but lo and behold, there are 2 separate sets of APIs:

  • CMIS Compliant – API Reference: Most of the APIs for working with content are compliant with CMIS, an industry standard for APIs that call content management systems. 
     
  • SAP REST API – API Reference: These have more to do with creating a repo and administrative tasks.

Objects

The contents of the repo are objects, which contain CMIS-defined properties. Some of the more notable properties:

  • cmis:objectId: Unique ID for that object
  • cmis:objectTypeId: Indicates the type of object, generally document (cmis:document) or folder (cmis:folder)
  • cmis:name: Name for that object
  • cmis:parentIds: Object ID of the folders that contain this object
  • ... you can also get the owner, creation date, modified date, version, mime type of document type and more. DMS has complex capabilities, like version or encryption, and those are beyond the scope of this blog post.

 

"properties": {
	"cmis:objectId": {
		"id": "cmis:objectId",
		"localName": "cmis:objectId",
		"displayName": "cmis:objectId",
		"queryName": "cmis:objectId",
		"type": "id",
		"cardinality": "single",
		"value": "DzX04u_1PD5uNT2IfcmNzztROjky-92pHkAK1-SoE5U"
	},
	"cmis:name": {
		"id": "cmis:name",
		"localName": "cmis:name",
		"displayName": "cmis:name",
		"queryName": "cmis:name",
		"type": "string",
		"cardinality": "single",
		"value": "invoice_id-1731489392337-28"
	},
	"cmis:objectTypeId": {
		"id": "cmis:objectTypeId",
		"localName": "cmis:objectTypeId",
		"displayName": "cmis:objectTypeId",
		"queryName": "cmis:objectTypeId",
		"type": "id",
		"cardinality": "single",
		"value": "cmis:folder"
	}
}

 

Note that the property names start with cmis. When working with the properties, you need to use this namespace. This affects how you write some of the URLs (such as the query call) as well as selecting properties in the formula editor of SAP Build Apps (see below).

SAP Build Apps Formula Editor

Since the property names have a colon in them (:), you will need to use an alternate form for specifying those fields in the formula editor of SAP Build Apps.

For example, if you use the standard format, the formula editor will give an error:

Instead, you need to put such properties inside parentheses:

Queries

All but one of the calls are standard REST calls, and similar to many other REST APIs. But there is a query call, that lets you write SQL-esque statements to retrieve objects. 

For example, if I want to retrieve all of the documents for a folder with a specific ID, and return only the name property, I could use the following URL: 

 

https://{{endpoint}}/browser/{{repo}}?cmisSelector=query&
q=SELECT cmis:name FROM cmis:folder WHERE
cmis:objectId='DzX04u_1PD5uNT2IfcmNzztROjky-92pHkAK1-SoE5U'

 

To get the full OASIS documentation on writing queries, see their documentation.

 

Postman Collection

You can download the collection here: https://github.com/sap-tutorials/sap-build-apps/blob/main/projects/dms/DMS_CMIS.postman_collection.json

In order to use the requests, you will need to set up an environment that supplies the following properties:

 

clientid
clientsecret
endpoint
authenticationurl
repo

 

The first 4 you will get from the service key of your DMS Integration Option service.

The service key will look something like this:

The repo you get when you run the API for all your repos:

IMPORTANT:

  • Some of the requests in the collection contain example objectIDs or file names, which of course will not work on your tenants. So you will have to change those.
